scenarios.description: parseInput(String, String) with non-empty HTML and valid baseUri returns a Document via treeBuilder.parse
scenarios.precondition: Use real HtmlTreeBuilder; no mocks needed
scenarios.expectedResult:
returns a Document whose body contains the parsed node for `

Test

`

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader, String) with empty input returns an empty Document
scenarios.precondition: Use StringReader for empty input; no mocks needed
scenarios.expectedResult: returns a Document with an empty body node list

scenarios.description: parseInput(Reader, String) when Reader throws IOException propagates UncheckedIOException
scenarios.precondition: Use stub Reader that throws IOException on read; mocking avoided by simple stub
pseudocode: Reader reader = new Reader() { public int read(char[] cbuf, int off, int len) throws IOException { throw new IOException("fail"); } public void close(){} };
